take place.example_sentence: The meeting will take place in the main office.
take place.original_sentence: Every day, it seems, this takes place in the fast-moving world of business.

fast-moving.original_sentence: Every day, it seems, this takes place in the fast-moving world of business.

accomplish.example_sentence: We need to accomplish this goal by the end of the week.
accomplish.original_sentence: The boss calls in an employee and explains that a special task must be accomplished.

handle.explanation_en: to deal with a situation or problem
handle.example_sentence: Can you handle the pressure of this new project?
handle.original_sentence: Then he says, I know you got a lot of work to do, but can you handle this?

loaded down.explanation_en: having too much work or responsibility
loaded down.example_sentence: I am too loaded down with homework to go to the movies.
loaded down.original_sentence: Too often the employee replies, I'm awfully sorry, but I'm all loaded down now.

take on.explanation_en: to agree to do some work or be responsible for something
take on.example_sentence: I cannot take on any more projects right now.
take on.original_sentence: I wish I could take it on, but I'm just too busy.

under the circumstances.example_sentence: Under the circumstances, we decided to cancel the outdoor event.
under the circumstances.original_sentence: Under the circumstances, the boss doesn't hold it against the employee because it is extra duty, so to speak.

hold it against.explanation_en: to blame someone or feel angry at them for something
hold it against.example_sentence: I made a mistake, but I hope you won't hold it against me.
hold it against.original_sentence: Under the circumstances, the boss doesn't hold it against the employee because it is extra duty, so to speak.

so to speak.explanation_en: used to explain that you are using words in a metaphorical or unusual way
so to speak.example_sentence: He is the father of the company, so to speak.
so to speak.original_sentence: Under the circumstances, the boss doesn't hold it against the employee because it is extra duty, so to speak.

forge ahead.explanation_en: to move forward or make progress
forge ahead.example_sentence: Despite the difficulties, the team decided to forge ahead with the plan.
forge ahead.original_sentence: And this employee is the fellow who will forge ahead.

output.explanation_en: the amount of something produced by a person or machine
output.example_sentence: The factory increased its output this month.
output.original_sentence: improve the quality of your output, and do more of what you do, increase the quantity of your output.

stand out.explanation_en: to be very easy to see or notice because of being better than others
stand out.example_sentence: Her hard work makes her stand out from the rest of the team.
stand out.original_sentence: Accepting greater responsibility on the job makes you stand out and shows that you're more valuable.

civic.explanation_en: relating to a city or the duties of people who live there
civic.example_sentence: Voting is an important civic duty.
civic.original_sentence: When your neighbors ask you to represent them on a civic matter, accept.

shortcut.explanation_en: a quicker or more direct way of doing something
shortcut.example_sentence: There are no shortcuts to learning a new language.
shortcut.original_sentence: or taking intelligent shortcuts in your routine activities, or possibly dropping non-essential activities altogether.

competent.explanation_en: having the ability to do something well
competent.example_sentence: She is a very competent manager.
competent.original_sentence: All the successful, competent people I know are busy.

count on.explanation_en: to trust someone to do something
count on.example_sentence: I can always count on my best friend for help.
count on.original_sentence: I have learned in dozens of instances that I can count on a busy man to deliver.

expand.explanation_en: to increase in size, number, or importance
expand.example_sentence: The company plans to expand its business to other countries.
expand.original_sentence: Progressive business management constantly asks, what can we do to expand output?
